sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Dana says sale of off-highway business will make stronger company
feed.summary: Dana Incorporated reported its second-quarter earnings, highlighting the sale of its off-highway business to Allison. This strategic move is intended to make Dana a more focused supplier to the light and commercial vehicle markets and substantially strengthens its balance sheet. The company also announced a $1 billion capital return authorization and expects significant cost savings through 2026.

feed.title: Donegal reports Q4 and full-year 2024 results
feed.summary: Donegal Insurance Group reported strong financial results for both Q4 and the full-year 2024, showing significant improvements in net income and combined ratio. The company attributed this success to premium growth, underwriting enhancements, and cost reduction efforts. CEO Kevin G. Burke emphasized ongoing strategies including targeted rate increases and new business development to sustain growth and boost shareholder value.

feed.title: Capital Wealth Management LLC Boosts Stake in GraniteShares Bloomberg Commodity Broad Strategy No K-1 ETF (NYSEARCA:COMB)
feed.summary: Capital Wealth Management LLC significantly increased its holdings in the GraniteShares Bloomberg Commodity Broad Strategy No K-1 ETF (NYSEARCA:COMB) by 41.7% during the third quarter, making it their 23rd largest investment. Several other hedge funds also adjusted their positions in COMB, with some acquiring new stakes and others boosting existing ones. The article also provides a brief overview of the ETF's recent stock performance.

feed.title: Invesco stock hits 52-week high at 28.64 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Invesco (IVZ) stock reached a new 52-week high of $28.64, reflecting an 82.36% increase over the past year. The asset management firm, with a market capitalization of $12.73 billion, offers a 2.94% dividend yield and has maintained payouts for 19 consecutive years. Recent developments include a slight decrease in assets under management, a restructuring of the Invesco QQQ Trust, and a raised price target from BMO Capital.

feed.title: Borgwarner stock hits 52-week high at $48.19 By Investing.com
feed.summary: BorgWarner Inc. stock recently reached a new 52-week high of $48.19, reflecting a 55.89% increase over the past year and a 35.7% gain in the last six months. This surge is attributed to the company's strategic focus on innovation and electric vehicle components, positioning it favorably in the automotive sector. Despite trading slightly below its Fair Value, BorgWarner maintains a "GOOD" financial health score and has consistently paid dividends for 13 years.

feed.title: Applied Materials stock rises on Susquehanna upgrade as chip gear spending call widens
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) shares rose 1.7% in early trading following an upgrade from Susquehanna to "Positive" with a price target increase from $180 to $400. The upgrade is based on anticipated increased spending in wafer fab equipment (WFE), with projections of $120 billion in current spending and potential for over $150 billion. Investors are now looking forward to upcoming CPI data and TSMC's earnings call for further market signals.

feed.title: Alphabet Becomes Newest $4 Trillion Company, Joining Nvidia and Apple
feed.summary: Alphabet's market capitalization surpassed $4 trillion in intraday trading, making it the latest tech giant to reach this milestone. This achievement is attributed to investor optimism surrounding its growing artificial intelligence business and the recent launch of its Gemini 3 AI model. Shares of Alphabet rose to a record $334.04 on Monday morning, reflecting strong growth of 65.4% last year.

feed.title: Netflix had a huge night at the 2026 Golden Globes with 7 wins
feed.summary: Netflix dominated the 2026 Golden Globes with seven wins, significantly ahead of other streaming platforms. Their hit series "Adolescence" secured four awards, and "KPop Demon Hunters" won best animated feature and best original song. This success comes as Netflix prepares for its acquisition of Warner Bros., highlighting its growing industry dominance.

feed.title: Mizuho Lowers Price Target for Digital Realty Trust (DLR) to $18
feed.summary: Mizuho has adjusted its price target for Digital Realty Trust (DLR) downward from $191.00 to $180.00, while maintaining an "Outperform" rating. This 5.76% decrease reflects a new assessment, even as other analysts have shown varied ratings and price target changes for the data center REIT in recent months. The average target price from 25 analysts is $194.60, implying a 23.41% upside from its current price, with the consensus brokerage recommendation being "Outperform".

feed.title: Mizuho Cuts Digital Realty Trust (NYSE:DLR) Price Target to $180.00
feed.summary: Mizuho has adjusted its price target for Digital Realty Trust (NYSE:DLR) from $191 to $180, while maintaining an "outperform" rating, indicating a potential 14.65% upside. The consensus among analysts remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $194.10. Despite the price target cut, institutional investors hold a significant 99.7% of the stock, and the company's shares are currently trading around $157.01, below the average analyst target.

feed.title: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) Price Target Raised to $152.00 at Mizuho
feed.summary: Mizuho has increased its price target for Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) from $150.00 to $152.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating and suggesting a nearly 9.9% upside. Other analysts have also adjusted their targets, contributing to a "Moderate Buy" consensus with an average price target of $146.94. The company's stock recently traded at $138.32, with insider sales totaling approximately 86,094 shares valued at about $11.96 million in the last 90 days.

feed.title: Is P&G (PG) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Procter & Gamble's (PG) investment potential based on Wall Street analyst recommendations versus Zacks Rank. While analysts show optimism for PG with an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) leaning towards "Strong Buy," the article highlights potential biases in these recommendations. It advocates for using the Zacks Rank, which is based on earnings estimate revisions, as a more reliable indicator for future stock price movements, noting that PG currently holds a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell) due to declining earnings estimates.

feed.title: iRhythm Technologies stock maintains Overweight rating at Morgan Stanley
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has maintained an Overweight rating and $205.00 price target on iRhythm Technologies (NASDAQ:IRTC) following strong preliminary fourth-quarter results, which exceeded consensus revenue estimates. The company reported robust year-over-year revenue growth and provided an optimistic outlook for fiscal year 2026, surpassing Street estimates, despite not yet expecting profitability this year. The forecast also does not include potential revenue from its next-generation Mobile Cardiac Telemetry device, which awaits FDA clearance and could significantly increase market share.

feed.title: American Tower (NYSE:AMT) Price Target Cut to $189.00 by Analysts at Mizuho
feed.summary: Mizuho analysts have lowered their price target for American Tower (NYSE:AMT) from $217.00 to $189.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, though the consensus among analysts remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average target of $223.32. The real estate investment trust recently reported earnings of $2.78 per share, exceeding analyst estimates, and traded up 1.0% during midday trading on Monday. Insider and institutional activity includes a significant share sale by Tower Corp and purchases by a director, with institutional investors owning 92.69% of the stock.

feed.title: Zacks Market Edge Highlights: Exxon Mobil, Chevron and ConocoPhillips
feed.summary: The Zacks Market Edge podcast, hosted by Tracey Ryniec, discusses whether oil stocks like Exxon Mobil (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and ConocoPhillips (COP) are values or traps for 2026, especially considering recent developments in Venezuela and consistently low oil prices. The analysis delves into the earnings outlook and financial metrics for each company, noting expected declines in earnings for 2025 and 2026 despite some stock price increases. The podcast aims to help investors determine if these major oil players are attractive investments given their current valuations and future prospects.

feed.title: Biogen Receives European Commission Approval for High Dose Regimen of SPINRAZA® (nusinersen) for Spinal Muscular Atrophy
feed.summary: Biogen has received European Commission marketing authorization for a high-dose regimen of SPINRAZA® (nusinersen) for the treatment of 5q spinal muscular atrophy (SMA). This approval is based on data from the DEVOTE study, which demonstrated improved motor function in both treatment-naïve and previously-treated SMA patients. The new regimen includes a more rapid loading phase and 28 mg maintenance doses every four months, offering an important advancement in SMA care.

feed.title: Higher-dose spinal muscular atrophy treatment approved for patients in EU
feed.summary: The European Commission has approved Biogen's high-dose SPINRAZA (nusinersen) regimen for 5q spinal muscular atrophy, the most common form of the disease. This updated dosing schedule involves two 50 mg loading doses followed by 28 mg maintenance injections every four months, supported by positive data from the DEVOTE Phase 2/3 study. The new regimen aims to address the evolving needs of SMA patients and is also under review by the U.S. FDA with a decision expected by April 3, 2026.

feed.title: 5 takeaways from Xcel CEO Bob Frenzel’s talk at the Minneapolis Fed
feed.summary: Xcel Energy CEO Bob Frenzel discussed the dramatic impact of data center growth on electric utilities at the Minneapolis Fed, highlighting multiple drivers of demand growth, degrading supply chains, and the need for alternative rate structures. He also advocated for nuclear energy as a preferred source for data centers and noted the significant rise in engineering, procurement, and construction costs due to competition from hyperscalers. The utility is adapting its strategies to manage this evolving landscape, including long-term contracts and advocating for national initiatives in nuclear power.

feed.title: Medtronic wins FDA clearance for MiniMed Go app for InPen smart insulin pen
feed.summary: Medtronic has received FDA 510(k) clearance for its MiniMed Go app, designed for multiple daily injections (MDI). This smart MDI system integrates the InPen smart insulin pen with Abbott's Instinct sensor, providing real-time personalized insights and guidance for users. The collaboration between Medtronic and Abbott, initiated in August 2024, aims to enhance insulin delivery and continuous glucose monitoring technologies, with the MiniMed Go app offering features like missed dose alerts and an integrated dose calculator.

feed.title: Peachtree refinances Atlanta hotel
feed.summary: Peachtree Group has refinanced the 265-key Candler Hotel in Atlanta, a property operating under Hilton's Curio Collection and managed by Highgate Hotels. JLL Hotels & Hospitality facilitated the transaction for the hotel, which was originally built in 1906 by Coca-Cola magnate Asa Griggs Candler and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places. The refinancing reflects lender confidence in the hotel's market position and Atlanta's recovery as a convention destination.

feed.title: Price Over Earnings Overview: CMS Energy
feed.summary: CMS Energy (NYSE: CMS) is trading at $70.51 after a 0.74% spike, with its stock up 0.86% over the past month and 7.55% over the past year. The company's P/E ratio of 20.17 is lower than the Multi-Utilities industry average of 26.17, which could indicate undervaluation or weaker growth prospects. Investors are advised to consider the P/E ratio alongside other financial metrics and industry trends for comprehensive decision-making.

feed.title: Accelsius Closes $65 Million Series B Funding Led by Johnson Controls; Legrand Joining Round to Scale Liquid Cooling for Gigawatt-Class AI Factories – Company Announcement - FT.com
feed.summary: Accelsius, a leader in two-phase, direct-to-chip liquid cooling for AI and high-performance computing, has closed a $65 million Series B funding round. The round was led by Johnson Controls, with strategic investment from Legrand, to scale liquid cooling solutions for AI factories. This investment will accelerate Accelsius' expansion, global reach, and the deployment of its proprietary NeuCool® technology, crucial for managing the extreme thermal demands of next-generation AI and HPC chips.

feed.title: Hershey and Shaq spark bold new era in playful confectionery
feed.summary: Hershey and Shaquille O’Neal have launched "Shaq-A-Licious SLAMS," a multi-textural gummy candy designed to encourage playful interaction. This new product features gummy balls and a sour mango hoop, signaling a growing trend in the confectionery industry towards experiential, interactive, and personalized sweets. The collaboration aims to blend childhood play with contemporary flavors, catering to consumers seeking fun and unexpected experiences in their snacks.

feed.title: Raymond James Adjusts Price Target on C.H. Robinson Worldwide to $185 From $161, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Raymond James has increased its price target for C.H. Robinson Worldwide to $185 from $161, reiterating an "Outperform" rating. This adjustment reflects an optimistic outlook from the firm regarding the company's future performance. The article also lists several other analyst rating updates for C.H. Robinson Worldwide from various financial institutions.

feed.title: BioXcel Therapeutics Appoints Mark Pavao as Interim Chief Commercial Officer
feed.summary: BioXcel Therapeutics has appointed Mark Pavao as Interim Chief Commercial Officer to lead the commercial planning and execution for IGALMI, particularly for its potential at-home use. The company plans to submit a supplemental New Drug Application (sNDA) this month for FDA approval of IGALMI for acute treatment of agitation associated with bipolar disorders or schizophrenia in an at-home setting. Pavao brings over 30 years of experience in CNS drug launches and brand building, aiming to maximize IGALMI's value and address unmet patient needs.

feed.title: Zacks Market Edge Highlights: Exxon Mobil, Chevron and ConocoPhillips
feed.summary: This Zacks Market Edge podcast episode discusses whether major oil stocks like Exxon Mobil, Chevron, and ConocoPhillips are value opportunities or "traps" as the oil industry enters 2026. Host Tracey Ryniec analyzes their P/E ratios, dividend yields, and future earnings forecasts in light of fluctuating oil prices and geopolitical developments, particularly concerning Venezuela. The episode aims to guide investors on potential plays in the oil sector for the coming year.

feed.title: Vertex Pharma Is Quietly Exploding – Here’s What Wall Street Isn’t Telling You
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) is gaining attention as a biotech "blue-chip" stock, quietly outperforming while investors focus on AI. The company's success is rooted in its dominance in cystic fibrosis treatments, providing strong cash flow to fund a promising pipeline in gene-editing and non-opioid pain relief. Despite its high price, it's considered a long-term conviction stock for its quality earnings and growth potential, appealing to investors looking for stability in the volatile biotech sector.

feed.title: Buy 5 Stocks With High ROE as U.S. Economy Appears on Firm Footing
feed.summary: Despite a slight dip in the job market, the U.S. economy remains strong, leading investors to seek "cash cow" stocks with high Return on Equity (ROE). This article identifies five such stocks—Arista Networks, Corning, Banco Bilbao, Assurant, and Host Hotels & Resorts—that demonstrate robust financial health and efficient reinvestment of cash, making them attractive investments in the current economic landscape. The selection criteria include strong cash flow, favorable price/cash flow ratios, high return on assets, and consistent EPS historical growth.

feed.title: Astronics Corporation stock hits all-time high of 67.59 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Astronics Corporation (ATRO) stock has reached an all-time high of $67.59, driven by strong performance and investor confidence in the aerospace sector. Although the stock is trading just below its 52-week high, InvestingPro analysis suggests it may be overvalued and in overbought territory. The company recently reported strong Q3 2025 earnings and received increased price targets from analysts, reflecting positive outlooks for its future growth.

feed.title: Cummins stock hits all-time high at 554.64 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Cummins Inc. (CMI) stock has reached an all-time high of $554.64 USD, surging 58.7% over the past year and 64.5% in the last six months. The company, with a market capitalization of $76 billion and a P/E ratio of 28.7, has received "GREAT" financial health from InvestingPro and maintained dividend payments for 55 consecutive years. Analysts have also upgraded their ratings on Cummins, citing strong performance and anticipated growth in various segments.

feed.title: Costamare Inc stock hits 52-week high at $16.40 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Costamare Inc. (CMRE) has reached a new 52-week high of $16.40, signifying a 28.65% increase over the past year and reflecting strong market performance and investor confidence. The company recently surpassed Q3 2025 earnings and revenue expectations, reporting an EPS of $0.81 and revenues of $225.17 million. Costamare also declared cash dividends on its preferred and common stocks, payable in January 2026.

feed.title: BNP Paribas Exane Adjusts Hershey Price Target to $224 From $220, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BNP Paribas Exane has increased its price target for Hershey (HSY) to $224 from $220, while reiterating an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook from the analyst firm for the confectionery giant. The report was published on January 12, 2026, at 10:45 am EST according to MT Newswires.

feed.title: BioNTech’s Strategic Pivot: A Deep Dive into Its Oncology Ambitions
feed.summary: BioNTech is undergoing a major strategic shift, moving from a COVID-19 vaccine-focused company to a diversified pharmaceutical firm with a strong emphasis on oncology. Despite declining COVID-19 vaccine revenue, BioNTech plans to initiate six new Phase 3 oncology trials in 2026, backed by a substantial cash reserve of €17.2 billion. The company anticipates a pivotal year in 2026, with seven late-stage clinical trial readouts expected to validate its new strategy and commercial potential in cancer therapies.

feed.title: Walmart Joins Nasdaq-100, Teams Up With Google, Shares Surge
feed.summary: Walmart's shares surged after the announcement that it will join the Nasdaq-100 index, replacing AstraZeneca PLC. Additionally, Walmart revealed a partnership with Google to integrate Gemini AI for product discovery and shopping, accessible through a Universal Commerce Protocol. This collaboration aims to revolutionize online shopping with personalized recommendations and expedient delivery options.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts EPAM Systems Price Target to $250 From $235, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has increased its price target for EPAM Systems (EPAM) to $250 from $235, reaffirming a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook from the investment bank for the IT services and consulting company. The article also lists recent analyst adjustments and company news related to EPAM Systems, including other firms maintaining Buy ratings and announcing strategic partnerships.

feed.title: Barclays Downgrades Insulet to Underweight From Equal Weight, Adjusts Price Target to $274 From $316
feed.summary: Barclays has downgraded Insulet (PODD) from Equal Weight to Underweight and significantly lowered its price target from $316 to $274. This reflects a less optimistic outlook from Barclays on the medical device company specializing in insulin-dependent diabetes management. The article also lists other recent analyst adjustments for Insulet, showing a mix of upgrades, downgrades, and price target changes from various financial institutions.

feed.title: CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP) Is Considered a Good Investment by Brokers: Is That True?
feed.summary: This article examines the reliability of brokerage recommendations for CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP), which currently has an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.96, suggesting a "Buy." However, it cautions against relying solely on ABR due to inherent positive bias from brokerage firms, instead advocating for the use of Zacks Rank, a proprietary stock rating tool. The article highlights key differences between ABR and Zacks Rank, emphasizing the latter's timeliness and correlation with earnings estimate revisions for more accurate investment decisions, noting CRSP currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: SMCI Plunges 45% in 3 Months: Time to Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock?
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) shares have dropped 44.9% in the last three months, leading to an attractive valuation. Despite facing near-term challenges like contracting gross margins and negative free cash flow due to rapid expansion and customer concentration, the company is strategically positioning itself to capitalize on the AI market with high-performance servers and new product lines. Therefore, the article suggests holding the stock for now, acknowledging both its challenges and growth potential.

feed.title: Moderna Hits 2025 Revenue Goal as Operating Costs Fall Faster than Forecast
feed.summary: Moderna announced unaudited full-year 2025 revenue of $1.9 billion, aligning with its target range and Wall Street consensus. The company also reduced its 2025 operating expense forecast to $5 billion to $5.2 billion, demonstrating faster-than-expected cost-cutting. Despite these positive updates, Jefferies analysts suggest more reductions are necessary for Moderna to confidently achieve its 2028 break-even target, projecting break-even might occur between 2029 and 2030.

feed.title: Lululemon hints it had a strong Christmas, says Q4 sales at high end of guidance
feed.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. announced that it expects its net revenue and diluted earnings per share for its fourth quarter to be at the high end of its guidance, signaling a strong holiday season performance. This positive outlook comes as the company faces a proxy battle and prepares for a change in leadership with CEO Calvin McDonald stepping down. Founder Chip Wilson and activist investor Elliott Management are pushing for significant changes to the board and CEO selection process, while Lululemon maintains confidence in its current board.

feed.title: Is Bank of America Stock Worth Owning Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
feed.summary: Bank of America (BAC) is set to announce its Q4 and full-year 2025 results on January 14, with analysts expecting strong revenue and earnings growth driven by net interest income (NII) and capital markets performance. While the company has an impressive earnings surprise history, its Earnings ESP suggests a low chance of beating estimates this quarter. Investors are advised to watch management's NII and investment banking outlook during the earnings call, and current shareholders are encouraged to hold for long-term potential.

feed.title: Zimmer Biomet Holdings (ZBH) Announces Executive Resignation
feed.summary: Zimmer Biomet Holdings (ZBH) announced the resignation of Mark Bezjak, a named executive officer, effective January 16, 2026. Bezjak is departing to pursue another business opportunity, having already ceased to be an executive officer as of July 1, 2025. This leadership change is significant for the musculoskeletal healthcare company and its stakeholders.

feed.title: American Electric Power (AEP) Receives Downgrade from B of A Sec
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) received a downgrade from 'Buy' to 'Neutral' by B of A Securities, with a price target reduction from $131.00 to $122.00 USD. This adjustment comes amidst varied analyst ratings, where UBS maintained a 'Sell', JP Morgan a 'Neutral', Jefferies upgraded to 'Buy', and Citigroup maintained a 'Neutral' in recent weeks. AEP, a large regulated utility, has an average analyst target price of $129.10, suggesting a 10.10% upside, but GuruFocus estimates a GF Value of $108.21, implying a 7.71% downside.

feed.title: AirNexis Launches To Take On Merck In Expanding PDE3/4i COPD Field
feed.summary: AirNexis has launched with the goal of competing with Merck & Co. in the growing PDE3/4i market for Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD). The article highlights the expanding interest in this therapeutic area, referencing Merck's acquisition of Verona and GSK's deal with Hengrui for similar inhibitors.

feed.title: Synchrony Financial stock slides on Trump’s 10% credit-card rate cap push — what to know now
feed.summary: Synchrony Financial shares dropped about 7% after former President Donald Trump called for a one-year, 10% cap on credit-card interest rates. This proposal could significantly affect lenders like Synchrony, which rely heavily on interest income, potentially leading them to tighten credit standards or increase fees. While the legal viability of such a cap is uncertain, investors are closely monitoring the situation as bank earnings season begins.

feed.title: The Midstream Renaissance: Why ONEOK is Positioned as the Energy Sector’s Top Growth and Yield Play for 2026
feed.summary: ONEOK (NYSE: OKE) is highlighted as a top growth and yield play in the energy sector for 2026, benefiting from strategic acquisitions and significant synergy realization. The company is poised for substantial EPS and Adjusted EBITDA growth, aiming to accelerate shareholder returns after deleveraging. This strong performance is driven by increased demand from AI data centers and U.S. LNG exports, positioning ONEOK as a key infrastructure provider in a favorable regulatory environment.

feed.title: Dynavax Technologies stock hits 52-week high at $15.68 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Dynavax Technologies (DVAX) stock has reached a new 52-week high of $15.68, reflecting a 25.32% increase over the past year and a 45.65% return in the last six months. The company, known for vaccine development, boasts a market capitalization of $1.78 billion and a strong current ratio of 7.62. Recent news includes strong Q3 2025 earnings and Sanofi's agreement to acquire Dynavax for $2.2 billion, a 39% premium over its closing share price on December 23, 2025.

feed.title: NVR Inc.: The Quiet Giant Redefining America’s Homebuilding Machine
feed.summary: NVR Inc. is presented as a "quiet giant" in the homebuilding industry, distinguished by its asset-light, highly optimized platform focused on efficiency and risk reduction. The article details NVR's core strategies: an asset-light land acquisition model, standardized construction with local flexibility, and an integrated mortgage banking arm (NVR Mortgage), all contributing to its valuation premium. It contrasts NVR's approach with major competitors like D.R. Horton, Lennar, and PulteGroup, highlighting NVR's emphasis on stability, disciplined capital allocation, and a system built for cyclical markets.

feed.title: Braskem discloses details of restructuring talks for Braskem Idesa debt By Investing.com
feed.summary: Braskem S.A. announced that its subsidiary Braskem Idesa S.A.P.I. has entered into restructuring talks for its 7.450% Senior Secured Notes due 2029 and 6.990% Senior Secured Notes due 2032. While discussions included proposals from both Braskem Idesa and investors, no agreement has been reached to date, with Braskem Idesa declining the investors' terms. The company will keep the market informed of future material developments.

feed.title: Cboe Global Markets stock hits all-time high at 263.23 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Cboe Global Markets stock reached an all-time high of $263.23, reflecting strong investor confidence and the company's growth in global financial markets. The company has a "GREAT" financial health score, a 22% return on equity, and a history of consistent dividend payments. This milestone follows record trading volumes in 2025 and recent analyst upgrades, although InvestingPro suggests the stock is trading slightly above its Fair Value.
